title: Unit 8 Quiz 5
question: Which of the following are typically associated with a liquidity trap? Select all that apply.
explanation: A liquidity trap is characterized by near-zero interest rates and the failure of conventional expansionary monetary policy. It typically occurs during deep recessions or depressions, so high unemployment and deflationary pressures are common accompanying features. Very high production costs are associated with supply shocks and cost-push inflation, not a liquidity trap.
